title: Churn-Signal Golden Set: 1,001 CFPB Complaints
subtitle: Hand-verified LLM eval set with zero-shot 3B-LLM predictions and rationales
description:
# Churn-Signal Golden Set — 1,001 CFPB Complaints

A hand-verified evaluation set for a genuinely hard NLP detection task: does a real consumer complaint narrative contain an **explicit churn signal** — the consumer states they closed or are closing the account, switched or are switching provider, or voices a definite intention/threat to leave?

This is a **detection** task, not a churn-prediction task. The label answers "does the text say it," not "will this customer leave."

## How the labels were made

Every row went through a two-step, anti-circularity process:

1. **Claude proposed** a label, an evidence quote, and a confidence score for each narrative (`proposed_label`, `evidence_quote`, `confidence`, `evidence_verbatim`, `labeler_model`).
2. **A human reviewed every single row** and either confirmed or overrode the proposal (`human_overrode`). Final override rate: 6 out of 1,001 (0.6%).

This two-step protocol exists specifically so that evaluating an LLM classifier against these labels isn't circular (agreement with the labeler, not real accuracy) — the classifier scored in the companion predictions file is a *different* model from a *different* company than the one that proposed labels.

**Single annotator.** No inter-rater agreement is measurable here — a real limitation, stated rather than hidden.

## Source data and provenance

Narratives come from the [CFPB Consumer Complaint Database](https://www.consumerfinance.gov/data-research/consumer-complaints/), a U.S. federal public dataset. CFPB states: *"All complaint data we publish is freely available for anyone to use, analyze, and build on"* and narratives are published only *"if the consumer opts to share it publicly and after the Bureau takes steps to remove personal information."* Sampling was deterministic (hash-ordered, seed-stable, reproducible), narratives filtered to 200-2000 characters, stratified across three products:

| Product | Rows | Positive | Rate |
|---|---|---|---|
| Checking or savings account | 417 | 22 | 5.3% |
| Credit card | 417 | 26 | 6.2% |
| Debt collection | 167 | 0 | 0.0% |
| **Total** | **1,001** | **48** | **4.8%** |

**A structural finding, not a sampling accident:** all 167 human-reviewed Debt collection rows are negative. A debt collector is an assigned relationship a consumer never chose, so "churn" from one is close to incoherent — sampling was deliberately capped there rather than grown until noise produced a positive.

## Intended use: EVALUATION

This set was built to evaluate classifiers (see the companion predictions files below), not to train them. Please don't train models on it directly — doing so would contaminate it as an evaluation benchmark for this task. The included predictions let you do real error analysis without running any model at all.

## Files

- **golden_set.csv** — the 1,001 labeled narratives with full labeling provenance.
- **llm_predictions.csv** — zero-shot predictions from a local 3B LLM (`llama3.2:3b`, `temperature=0`, JSON-schema structured output), one row per narrative, each with the model's own one-sentence rationale and per-call generation time.
- **tfidf_predictions.csv** — predictions from a classical TF-IDF + Logistic Regression baseline.
- **cv_folds.csv** — the canonical 5-fold stratified cross-validation split (every row is scored exactly once as a held-out case across the 5 folds) used to score both models identically.

Both prediction files share `cv_folds.csv`'s fold assignment, so the headline comparison is reproducible in seconds, no GPU required:

| Metric | TF-IDF + LR | LLM zero-shot (llama3.2:3b) |
|---|---|---|
| Accuracy | 0.949 ± 0.013 | 0.871 ± 0.018 |
| Precision | 0.387 ± 0.264 | 0.220 ± 0.031 |
| Recall | 0.213 ± 0.122 | 0.647 ± 0.040 |
| F1 | 0.272 ± 0.166 | 0.327 ± 0.036 |

See the starter notebook attached to this dataset for the full walkthrough (live TF-IDF training, confusion matrices, error analysis, and a measured cost comparison).

## Full methodology

Full write-up, including the labeling rubric, edge-case taxonomy, LLM-as-judge bias measurements, and a real cost/throughput comparison: [github.com/EnigmaDevelop/ai-engineering, Chapter 3](https://github.com/EnigmaDevelop/ai-engineering/tree/main/chapters/03-eval-harness).

## License

CC BY 4.0. The underlying CFPB narrative text is U.S. federal public domain data, already scrubbed and published by CFPB. The labels, provenance fields, model predictions, and fold assignments are this project's own annotations, licensed under attribution terms.
userSpecifiedSources: CFPB Consumer Complaint Database (https://www.consumerfinance.gov/data-research/consumer-complaints/), a U.S. federal public dataset. Narratives are published by CFPB after consumer consent and personal-information scrubbing. Labels, model predictions, and fold assignments are this project's own annotations (see https://github.com/EnigmaDevelop/ai-engineering/tree/main/chapters/03-eval-harness).

resources.description: 1,001 human-verified CFPB complaint narratives with churn-signal labels and full AI-propose/human-review provenance.

fields.name: complaint_id
fields.description: CFPB's own complaint identifier.

fields.name: product
fields.description: CFPB product category (Checking or savings account, Credit card, or Debt collection).

fields.name: narrative
fields.description: The consumer's complaint narrative text, as published by CFPB (personal information already removed by CFPB).

fields.name: proposed_label
fields.description: Claude's proposed churn_signal label (0/1) before human review.

fields.name: evidence_quote
fields.description: The quote Claude cited from the narrative to justify its proposed label.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Claude's self-reported confidence in its proposal (high/low).

fields.name: evidence_verbatim
fields.description: Whether the evidence_quote is an exact substring of the narrative.

fields.name: labeler_model
fields.description: The model used for pre-labeling proposals (claude -p, subscription access).

fields.name: churn_signal
fields.description: FINAL human-verified label. 1 iff the narrative explicitly states the consumer closed/is closing the account, switched/is switching provider, or states a definite intention or threat to leave; 0 otherwise.

fields.name: ambiguous
fields.description: Rows the human annotator flagged as too ambiguous to label (none in the final set).

fields.name: human_overrode
fields.description: True where the final human label (churn_signal) differs from Claude's proposed_label. 6/1001 rows.

resources.description: Zero-shot predictions from a local llama3.2:3b model, one row per narrative in golden_set.csv.

fields.name: complaint_id
fields.description: Joins to golden_set.csv.

fields.name: product
fields.description: CFPB product category.

fields.name: fold
fields.description: CV fold (0-4) this row was scored in — matches cv_folds.csv.

fields.name: true_label
fields.description: The golden_set.csv churn_signal value for this row, copied here for convenience.

fields.name: predicted_label
fields.description: The LLM's predicted churn_signal (0/1).

fields.name: parse_failed
fields.description: True if the model's structured-output JSON failed to parse (defaults predicted_label to 0 when true).

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: The model's own one-sentence rationale for its verdict, generated AFTER the boolean verdict (JSON schema field order = generation order) — some rationales may be post-hoc justifications.

fields.name: eval_duration_ms
fields.description: Model generation time for this row in milliseconds, on the reference machine (CPU-bound, see repo ADR-0001 for hardware specs).

resources.description: Predictions from a TF-IDF + Logistic Regression baseline, one row per narrative in golden_set.csv.

fields.name: complaint_id
fields.description: Joins to golden_set.csv.

fields.name: fold
fields.description: CV fold (0-4) this row was scored in — matches cv_folds.csv.

fields.name: predicted_label
fields.description: The TF-IDF+LR model's predicted churn_signal (0/1), predicted on this fold's held-out split.

resources.description: Canonical 5-fold stratified cross-validation assignment — both prediction files share this split so their metrics are directly comparable.

fields.name: complaint_id
fields.description: Joins to golden_set.csv.

fields.name: fold
fields.description: Fold index, 0-4. Every complaint_id appears in exactly one fold.
